---

## Configuration

### Environment Variables (with defaults)
```
VITE_GITHUB_PAT=          # GitHub Personal Access Token
VITE_LLM_API_KEY=         # LLM API Key
VITE_GITHUB_INSTANCE=     # "github.com" or URL for GHES
VITE_LLM_BACKEND=         # "openai" or "litellm"
VITE_LLM_ENDPOINT=        # API endpoint URL
VITE_LLM_MODEL=           # Model name
VITE_DEMO_MODE=           # "true" or "false"
```

### Runtime Override
Settings dialog allows overriding env vars at runtime (persisted to localStorage).

---

## Architecture

### Services
```
src/services/
├── github.ts      # Fetch PRs, details, diffs, comments (supports GHES)
├── llm.ts         # Unified interface for OpenAI v1 API + LiteLLM Proxy
└── dummyData.ts   # Sample PR data for demo mode
```

### Redux Store
```
src/store/
├── index.ts              # Store configuration
├── hooks.ts              # Typed useAppDispatch / useAppSelector
└── slices/
    ├── configSlice.ts    # App config (env defaults + runtime overrides)
    ├── prsSlice.ts       # Current PR data
    └── chatSlice.ts      # Messages + streaming state
```

### Components
```
src/components/
├── SettingsDialog.tsx   # Runtime config override
├── Sidebar.tsx          # Repo/PR navigation (or demo mode toggle)
├── ChatWindow.tsx       # Chat messages + input
└── PRViewer.tsx         # File diffs, metadata
```

---

## Data Types

### GitHub PR
```typescript
interface PullRequest {
  id: number;
  number: number;
  title: string;
  body: string | null;
  state: 'open' | 'closed';
  merged: boolean;
  merged_at: string | null;
  user: GitHubUser;
  created_at: string;
  updated_at: string;
  head: { ref: string; sha: string; repo: { full_name: string } | null };
  base: { ref: string; sha: string; repo: { full_name: string } };
  url: string;
  html_url: string;
  diff_url: string;
  additions: number;
  deletions: number;
  changed_files: number;
  comments: number;
  review_comments: number;
  commits: number;
  labels: Array<{ id: number; name: string; color: string }>;
  requested_reviewers: GitHubUser[];
}
```

### Chat Message
```typescript
interface ChatMessage {
  id: string;
  role: 'user' | 'assistant' | 'system';
  content: string;
  timestamp: number;
  isStreaming?: boolean;
  error?: string;
}
```

### App Config
```typescript
interface AppConfig {
  githubPat: string;
  llmApiKey: string;
  githubInstance: string;
  llmBackend: 'openai' | 'litellm';
  llmEndpoint: string;
  llmModel: string;
  demoMode: boolean;
}
```

---

## Error Handling

### LLM API Errors
- Invalid API key: Error shown in message bubble
- Rate limiting: Error shown in message bubble
- Network errors: Error shown in message bubble
- Streaming errors: Graceful fallback with error message

### GitHub API Errors (when enabled)
- Invalid PAT: Prompt to update token
- Rate limiting: Show remaining requests
- Not found (404): Repository/PR not found

---

### ⏳ Phase 8: Code Quality & UX Improvements (PENDING)

Identified during initial code review. These are non-blocking improvements to address after the Phase 7 polish work.

#### Quality
- [x] **ESLint v9 flat config** (`eslint.config.js`) — Added repository-level flat config so `npm run lint` works with ESLint 9.
- [x] **`getPRFiles` pagination** (`src/services/github.ts:96`) — The GitHub API caps file lists at 100 per page. PRs with >100 changed files silently return incomplete data. Add pagination (loop until `response.data.length < perPage`) or at minimum show a warning banner in `PRViewer` when the file count equals the page limit.
- [x] **Stable React keys in `DiffView`** (`src/components/PRViewer.tsx:41`) — Diff lines currently use array index as `key`. Replace with a content-based key (e.g. `` `${i}-${line.slice(0, 8)}` ``) to prevent React reusing DOM nodes incorrectly when switching between files.

#### UX
- [x] **Persist dark mode preference** (`src/App.tsx:10`) — Dark mode state resets on every page reload. Initialise from `localStorage` and respect the OS `prefers-color-scheme` media query:
  ```ts
  const [darkMode, setDarkMode] = useState(
    () => localStorage.getItem('darkMode') === 'true'
      || window.matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: dark)').matches
  );
  ```
  Also persist the toggle: `localStorage.setItem('darkMode', String(!darkMode))` inside `toggleDarkMode`.
- [x] **Wire up the Refresh button** (`src/components/Sidebar.tsx:111`) — The refresh `<button>` in the sidebar is rendered in GitHub mode but has no `onClick` handler. Either connect it to a `fetchPullRequests` thunk (part of Phase 7 GitHub UI work) or hide it with `{false && ...}` until that feature is ready, to avoid confusing users with a dead control.

state.output:
<path>/home/mark/sandbox/personal/pr-review-chatbot/openspec/changes/repository-watcher/design.md</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: ## Context
2: 
3: This is a web application (React + Redux) that allows users to review GitHub PRs. Currently users must manually enter a full PR URL each time. The feature adds repository watching and PR list display.
4: 
5: ## Goals / Non-Goals
6: 
7: **Goals:**
8: - Add watched repositories list to sidebar with add/remove functionality
9: - Persist watched repos in localStorage
10: - Make PR number optional - load all PRs from a repo when empty
11: - Add PR list view in the left `PR Review` pane, with back navigation to the controls/watchlist view
12: 
13: **Non-Goals:**
14: - Real-time polling (manual refresh only)
15: - Notification system
16: - Filtering PRs by requested reviewer
17: - Webhook-based updates (would require server-side)
18: 
19: ## Decisions
20: 
21: ### 1. Storage: localStorage
22: 
23: Using localStorage (same as existing config) rather than Redux persist or a new backend service.
24: 
25: - **Rationale**: Simple, works with current architecture, no server changes needed
26: - **Alternative**: Redux persist - adds dependency, more complex
27: 
28: ### 2. Badge: Total open PRs
29: 
30: Displaying total count of open PRs rather than "unread" tracking.
31: 
32: - **Rationale**: Simpler to implement, sufficient for MVP
33: - **Alternative**: Unread tracking - would require storing seen PR IDs per repo
34: 
35: ### 3. PR List: Separate component from PRViewer
36: 
37: Creating a new `PRList` component rather than modifying `PRViewer`.
38: 
39: - **Rationale**: Keeps PRViewer focused on single PR detail, cleaner separation
40: - **Alternative**: Modify PRViewer to handle both - would add complexity
41: 
42: ### 4. Watch icon: Immediate add on click
43: 
44: Adding repo to watch list immediately when clicking the watch icon.
45: 
46: - **Rationale**: Simpler UX, user can easily remove if added by mistake
47: - **Alternative**: Confirmation dialog - adds friction
48: 
49: ### 5. Sidebar navigation: two views in the same pane
50: 
51: The left `PR Review` pane will behave as a small navigator with two views:
52: 
53: - **Controls view**: repository input, optional PR number input, load button, watchlist
54: - **Repo list view**: list of PRs for the active repository, plus a Back action
55: 
56: Behavior details:
57: 
58: - Clicking **Load PR** with a PR number switches to repo list view containing one selected PR (demo-like behavior)
59: - Clicking **Load All PRs** (empty PR number) switches to repo list view containing all open PRs
60: - Clicking a watched repository switches to repo list view for that repository
61: - Back returns to controls view and keeps form inputs intact
62: 
63: - **Rationale**: Matches existing demo mode mental model and keeps repository workflows in one consistent sidebar location
64: - **Alternative**: Render PR list in center chat area or right PR detail pane, which fragments navigation and blurs responsibility
65: 
66: ## Risks / Trade-offs
67: 
68: | Risk | Mitigation |
69: |------|------------|
70: | GitHub API rate limits when fetching PR counts for many repos | Limit number of watched repos (reasonable for personal use) |
71: | Invalid repos entered by user | Validate via API before adding, show error |
72: | Large number of PRs in a repo | Pagination in list view if needed |
73: | No real-time updates | Manual refresh only for MVP |
74: | Users may miss how to return from repo list view | Add visible Back action at top of repo list view |

(End of file - total 74 lines)
</content>

## Context

This is a web application (React + Redux) that allows users to review GitHub PRs. Currently users must manually enter a full PR URL each time. The feature adds repository watching and PR list display.

## Goals / Non-Goals

**Goals:**
- Add watched repositories list to sidebar with add/remove functionality

- Webhook-based updates (would require server-side)

## Decisions

<content>1: ## ADDED Requirements
2: 
3: ### Requirement: PR Number field is optional
4: The system SHALL allow loading PRs without specifying a PR number.
5: 
6: #### Scenario: Load all PRs without PR number
7: - **WHEN** user enters a repository in the repository input field
8: - **AND** leaves the PR Number field empty
9: - **AND** clicks "Load All PRs"
10: - **THEN** the system SHALL fetch all open PRs from that repository
11: - **AND** display them in a PR list view in the left `PR Review` pane
12: 
13: #### Scenario: Load single PR into list view when PR number is present
14: - **WHEN** user enters a repository in the repository input field
15: - **AND** enters a valid PR number
16: - **AND** clicks "Load PR"
17: - **THEN** the system SHALL switch the left `PR Review` pane to PR list view
18: - **AND** the list SHALL contain the specified PR as a selected list item
19: 
20: ### Requirement: User can view list of PRs from a repository
21: The system SHALL display a list of PRs when loaded without a specific PR number.
22: 
23: #### Scenario: Display PR list in left PR Review pane
24: - **WHEN** user has loaded all PRs from a repository
25: - **THEN** the left `PR Review` pane SHALL show a list of PRs
26: - **AND** each item SHALL display: PR number, title, author, and time since update
27: 
28: ### Requirement: User can select a PR from the list
29: The system SHALL allow users to view details of a specific PR from the list.
30: 
31: #### Scenario: Click PR to view details
32: - **WHEN** user clicks on a PR in the list
33: - **THEN** the system SHALL switch to detail view
34: - **AND** show files, comments, reviews, and chat for that PR
35: 
36: ### Requirement: Button text changes based on input
37: The system SHALL change the button text based on whether a PR number is entered.
38: 
39: #### Scenario: Button shows "Load PR" with PR number
40: - **WHEN** user has entered a PR number
41: - **THEN** the button SHALL display "Load PR"
42: 
43: #### Scenario: Button shows "Load All PRs" without PR number
44: - **WHEN** PR Number field is empty
45: - **THEN** the button SHALL display "Load All PRs"
46: 
47: ### Requirement: User can navigate back to controls view
48: The system SHALL allow users to return from PR list view to the controls/watchlist view in the left sidebar pane.
49: 
50: #### Scenario: Return from PR list to controls/watchlist
51: - **WHEN** user is viewing repository PR list in the left `PR Review` pane
52: - **AND** clicks the Back action
53: - **THEN** the system SHALL show the controls/watchlist view in the same pane

(End of file - total 53 lines)
</content>

<content>1: ## ADDED Requirements
2: 
3: ### Requirement: User can add a repository to watch list
4: The system SHALL allow users to add a GitHub repository to their watched repositories list by clicking a watch icon next to the repository input field.
5: 
6: #### Scenario: Add valid repository to watch list
7: - **WHEN** user enters a valid "owner/repo" in the repository input field
8: - **AND** clicks the watch icon button
9: - **THEN** the repository SHALL be added to the watched repos list
10: - **AND** the watch icon SHALL indicate the repository is being watched (filled icon)
11: 
12: #### Scenario: Cannot add invalid repository
13: - **WHEN** user enters an invalid or non-existent repository
14: - **AND** clicks the watch icon
15: - **THEN** the system SHALL display an error message
16: - **AND** the repository SHALL NOT be added to the watch list
17: 
18: ### Requirement: User can remove a repository from watch list
19: The system SHALL allow users to remove a repository from their watched list.
20: 
21: #### Scenario: Remove repository from watch list
22: - **WHEN** user clicks the remove (X) button next to a watched repository
23: - **THEN** the repository SHALL be removed from the watched list
24: 
25: ### Requirement: Watch list persists across sessions
26: The system SHALL persist the watched repositories list in localStorage.
27: 
28: #### Scenario: Watched repos persist after page reload
29: - **WHEN** user has added repositories to their watch list
30: - **AND** reloads the page
31: - **THEN** the watched repositories SHALL be restored from localStorage
32: 
33: ### Requirement: Watched repos show PR count badge
34: The system SHALL display a badge showing the count of open PRs for each watched repository.
35: 
36: #### Scenario: Badge shows open PR count
37: - **WHEN** watched repositories are displayed
38: - **THEN** each repository SHALL show a badge with the count of open PRs
39: - **AND** the count SHALL be fetched from the GitHub API when the sidebar loads or refresh is clicked
40: 
41: ### Requirement: User can open a watched repository PR list in-place
42: The system SHALL open repository PR list view in the same left `PR Review` pane when a watched repository is selected.
43: 
44: #### Scenario: Open watched repository PR list
45: - **WHEN** user clicks a watched repository item
46: - **THEN** the system SHALL switch the left `PR Review` pane to PR list view for that repository
47: - **AND** display open PRs for that repository

(End of file - total 47 lines)
</content>
state.title: openspec/changes/repository-watcher/specs/watched-repos/spec.md

metadata.preview:
## ADDED Requirements

### Requirement: User can add a repository to watch list
The system SHALL allow users to add a GitHub repository to their watched repositories list by clicking a watch icon next to the repository input field.

#### Scenario: Add valid repository to watch list
- **WHEN** user enters a valid "owner/repo" in the repository input field
- **AND** clicks the watch icon button
- **THEN** the repository SHALL be added to the watched repos list
- **AND** the watch icon SHALL indicate the repository is being watched (filled icon)

#### Scenario: Cannot add invalid repository
- **WHEN** user enters an invalid or non-existent repository
- **AND** clicks the watch icon
- **THEN** the system SHALL display an error message
- **AND** the repository SHALL NOT be added to the watch list

### Requirement: User can remove a repository from watch list
The system SHALL allow users to remove a repository from their watched list.

parts.text:
Done — I captured your decision in the OpenSpec artifacts and kept this as spec/design work only (no feature implementation yet).

- Updated scope wording to place PR list in the left `PR Review` pane in `openspec/changes/repository-watcher/proposal.md`.
- Added explicit two-view sidebar navigation (controls/watchlist vs repo list) and Back behavior in `openspec/changes/repository-watcher/design.md`.
- Aligned task breakdown with sidebar-first list UX in `openspec/changes/repository-watcher/tasks.md`.
- Updated requirements/scenarios for:
  - list rendering location,
  - `Load PR` with PR number -> one selected list item,
  - Back to controls/watchlist,
  in `openspec/changes/repository-watcher/specs/repo-pr-list/spec.md`.
- Added watched-repo click -> open in-place repo list requirement in `openspec/changes/repository-watcher/specs/watched-repos/spec.md`.
- Kept planning/agent context in sync in `plans/plan.md` and `agents.md`.

Validation I ran:
- `npm run lint`
- `npm run build`
- `npm run test`

All passed (with existing non-blocking warnings during test/build output).  

If you want, next I can start implementing this updated `repository-watcher` behavior.
